---
title: "Part C"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# Libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(survey)
library(gtsummary)
library(tableone)
library(here)

# Dataset 
nhanes_clean <- readRDS(here("data", "processed", "nhanes_bmd_pediatric_master.rds"))

# Verification
cat("--- DATASET VERIFICATION ---\n")
cat("Total Raw Sample Loaded (N): ", nrow(nhanes_clean), "\n")
cat("Analytic Subpopulation (subpop1 == 1): ", sum(nhanes_clean$subpop1 == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
```

```{r}
## Calculated weighted quantiles for exposure
# Temporary survey design to extract weighted quantiles within subpop
temp_design <- svydesign(
  id = ~SDMVPSU,
  strata = ~SDMVSTRA,
  weights = ~WTMEC6YR,
  nest = TRUE,
  data = nhanes_clean) 

#set subpop equal to our analytic sample
subpop_temp <- subset(temp_design, subpop1 ==1)

# Extract weighted 25th, 50th, and 75th percentiles of total screen time
screen_quantiles <- svyquantile(
  ~total_screen_time,
  design = subpop_design,
  quantiles = c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75),
  na.rm = TRUE
)


# Cutpoints
q25 <- as.numeric(screen_quantiles$total_screen_time[1, "quantile"])
q50 <- as.numeric(screen_quantiles$total_screen_time[2, "quantile"])
q75 <- as.numeric(screen_quantiles$total_screen_time[3, "quantile"])

print(screen_quantiles)
```

```{r}
#create new var for screen time quartiles and set as factor
nhanes_newvars <- nhanes_clean %>%
  mutate(
    screen_quart = case_when(
      total_screen_time <= q25 ~ "Q1",
      total_screen_time >q25 & total_screen_time <= q50 ~ "Q2",
      total_screen_time >q50 & total_screen_time <= q75 ~ "Q3",
      total_screen_time >q75 ~ "Q4",
    .default = NA_character_) %>%
      factor(levels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4")))

#unweighted sample size per quartile, restricted to subpop1: >4,600 in each
table(nhanes_newvars$screen_quart[nhanes_newvars$subpop1 == 1], useNA = "always")

#create new var for FPL categories
nhanes_newvars <- nhanes_newvars %>%
  mutate(
    fpl_cat = case_when(
      INDFMPIR < 1.30 ~ "Low Income (< 130% PIR)",
      INDFMPIR >= 1.30 & INDFMPIR <= 3.50 ~ "Middle Income (130-350% PIR)",
      INDFMPIR > 3.50 ~ "High Income (> 350% PIR)",
      .default = NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Low Income (< 130% PIR)", "Middle Income (130-350% PIR)", "High Income (> 350% PIR)")))

# check to make sure no missing values for z scores 
sum(nhanes_clean$subpop1 == 1 & is.na(nhanes_clean$bmd_zscore_adj))
#Create categorical version of TBLH BMD z-scores for prevalence estimates
nhanes_newvars <- nhanes_newvars %>%
  mutate(
    bmd_z_cat = case_when(
      bmd_zscore_adj <= -2.0 ~ "Low TBLH BMD",
      bmd_zscore_adj > -2.0 ~ "Normal TBLH BMD",
      .default = NA_character_
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("Normal TBLH BMD", "Low TBLH BMD")))

#confirm factors in nhanes_newvars
nhanes_newvars %>% 
  select(RIAGENDR, RIDRETH3, fpl_cat, screen_quart, bmd_z_cat, FSDCH) %>% 
  glimpse()
```

```{r}
## Primary survey design
nhanes_design_full <- svydesign(
  id = ~SDMVPSU,
  strata = ~SDMVSTRA,
  weights = ~WTMEC6YR,
  nest = TRUE,
  data = nhanes_newvars)

# Isolating analytic subpop
subpop_design <- subset(nhanes_design_full, subpop1 == 1)
```

```{r}
## Prevalence Estiimate ##
#Estimate prevalence of low BMD (defined as z score less than -2.0) using weighted estimates
svyciprop(~I(bmd_z_cat == "Low TBLH BMD"),
          design=subpop_design,
          method = "logit",
          level = 0.95)
```

```{r}
library(gtsummary)
library(survey)

# Subset designs by sex
design_males   <- subset(subpop_design, RIAGENDR %in% c("Male", "male", 1))
design_females <- subset(subpop_design, RIAGENDR %in% c("Female", "female", 2))

# Table 1 Variables
tb1_vars <- c("RIDAGEYR", "RIDRETH3", "parent_edu", "fpl_cat", "FSDCH")

#Table 1 - MALES
table1_males <- design_males %>%
  tbl_svysummary(
    by = screen_quart, 
    include = all_of(tb1_vars),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous()  ~ "{mean} ({sd})", 
      all_categorical() ~ "{n_unweighted} ({p}%)"
    ),
    digits = all_categorical() ~ c(0, 1)
  ) %>%
  add_overall(col_label = "**Overall** (N = {N_unweighted})") %>%
  bold_labels()

table1_males

#Table 1 - FEMALES
table1_females <- design_females %>%
  tbl_svysummary(
    by = screen_quart, 
    include = all_of(tb1_vars),
    statistic = list(
      all_continuous()  ~ "{mean} ({sd})", 
      all_categorical() ~ "{n_unweighted} ({p}%)"
    ),
    digits = all_categorical() ~ c(0, 1)
  ) %>%
  add_overall(col_label = "**Overall** (N = {N_unweighted})") %>%
  bold_labels()

table1_females
```

```{r}
#create subset for males and females (unweighted)
unweighted_males <- nhanes_newvars %>%
  filter(subpop1 == 1 & RIAGENDR == "Male")

unweighted_females <- nhanes_newvars %>%
  filter(subpop1 == 1 & RIAGENDR == "Female")

nrow(unweighted_males) #verify numbers
nrow(unweighted_females)

#Unweighted counts
unweighted_males %>%
  freqtables::freq_table(screen_quart, RIDRETH3) %>%
  select(
    screen_quart = row_cat, 
    race_ethnicity = col_cat, 
    n, 
    percent = percent_row
  )
```
